# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent {
        label 'master'
    }

    environment {
        APP_NAME = 'my-application'
        DEPLOY_DIR = '/opt/deployments'
        BUILD_DIR = 'build'
        TEST_REPORT_DIR = 'test-reports'
    }

    options {
        timestamps()
        timeout(time: 30, unit: 'MINUTES')
        buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '10'))
        disableConcurrentBuilds()
    }

    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                echo "Checking out source code on master node..."
                checkout scm
            }
        }

        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                echo "Building ${APP_NAME} on master node..."
                sh """
                    mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}
                    echo "Compiling application..."
                    # Example build commands - replace with actual build tool
                    # For Maven:
                    # mvn clean package -DskipTests
                    # For Gradle:
                    # ./gradlew build -x test
                    # For Node.js:
                    # npm ci
                    # npm run build
                    echo "Build completed successfully"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Unit Tests') {
            steps {
                echo "Running unit tests on master node..."
                sh """
                    mkdir -p ${TEST_REPORT_DIR}
                    echo "Executing unit test suite..."
                    # Example test commands - replace with actual test runner
                    # For Maven:
                    # mvn test
                    # For Gradle:
                    # ./gradlew test
                    # For Node.js:
                    # npm run test -- --ci --reporters=default --reporters=jest-junit
                    echo "Unit tests passed"
                """
            }
            post {
                always {
                    echo "Collecting test reports..."
                    // junit allowEmptyResults: true, testResults: "${TEST_REPORT_DIR}/**/*.xml"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Integration Tests') {
            steps {
                echo "Running integration tests on master node..."
                sh """
                    echo "Executing integration test suite..."
                    # Example integration test commands
                    # mvn verify -Pintegration-tests
                    # ./gradlew integrationTest
                    # npm run test:integration
                    echo "Integration tests passed"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Code Quality Analysis') {
            steps {
                echo "Running code quality checks on master node..."
                sh """
                    echo "Performing static analysis..."
                    # Example quality check commands
                    # mvn sonar:sonar
                    # ./gradlew sonarqube
                    # npm run lint
                    echo "Code quality checks passed"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Package Artifact') {
            steps {
                echo "Packaging artifact on master node..."
                sh """
                    echo "Creating deployment artifact..."
                    # Example packaging commands
                    # tar -czf ${BUILD_DIR}/${APP_NAME}-${BUILD_NUMBER}.tar.gz -C ${BUILD_DIR} .
                    # docker build -t ${APP_NAME}:${BUILD_NUMBER} .
                    echo "Artifact packaged successfully"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy to Staging') {
            steps {
                echo "Deploying to staging environment from master node..."
                sh """
                    echo "Deploying ${APP_NAME} build #${BUILD_NUMBER} to staging..."
                    # Example staging deployment commands
                    # kubectl apply -f k8s/staging/ --namespace=staging
                    # ansible-playbook deploy-staging.yml
                    # scp ${BUILD_DIR}/${APP_NAME}-${BUILD_NUMBER}.tar.gz staging-server:/opt/apps/
                    echo "Staging deployment completed"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Smoke Tests') {
            steps {
                echo "Running smoke tests against staging..."
                sh """
                    echo "Verifying staging deployment health..."
                    # Example smoke test commands
                    # curl -f http://staging.example.com/health || exit 1
                    # npm run test:smoke -- --env=staging
                    echo "Smoke tests passed"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            when {
                branch 'main'
            }
            steps {
                echo "WARNING: Deploying to PRODUCTION from master node!"
                input message: 'Approve production deployment?', ok: 'Deploy to Production'
                sh """
                    echo "Deploying ${APP_NAME} build #${BUILD_NUMBER} to production..."
                    mkdir -p ${DEPLOY_DIR}
                    # Example production deployment commands
                    # kubectl apply -f k8s/production/ --namespace=production
                    # ansible-playbook deploy-production.yml
                    # scp ${BUILD_DIR}/${APP_NAME}-${BUILD_NUMBER}.tar.gz prod-server:/opt/apps/
                    echo "Production deployment completed successfully"
                """
            }
        }

        stage('Post-Deployment Verification') {
            when {
                branch 'main'
            }
            steps {
                echo "Verifying production deployment..."
                sh """
                    echo "Running production health checks..."
                    # Example verification commands
                    # curl -f http://production.example.com/health || exit 1
                    # npm run test:smoke -- --env=production
                    echo "Production verification passed"
                """
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        success {
            echo "Pipeline completed successfully for ${APP_NAME} build #${BUILD_NUMBER}"
            // mail to: 'team@example.com',
            //      subject: "SUCCESS: ${APP_NAME} Build #${BUILD_NUMBER}",
            //      body: "The pipeline completed successfully. Build #${BUILD_NUMBER} has been deployed."
        }
        failure {
            echo "Pipeline FAILED for ${APP_NAME} build #${BUILD_NUMBER}"
            // mail to: 'team@example.com',
            //      subject: "FAILURE: ${APP_NAME} Build #${BUILD_NUMBER}",
            //      body: "The pipeline has failed. Please check the Jenkins console output for details."
        }
        unstable {
            echo "Pipeline is UNSTABLE for ${APP_NAME} build #${BUILD_NUMBER}"
        }
        always {
            echo "Cleaning up workspace on master node..."
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
